headlines.title: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says
headlines.description: The US secretary of state earlier said that an agreement could possibly come on Monday.

headlines.title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
headlines.description: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

headlines.title: Greek swimmer only athlete to beat world record at controversial Enhanced Games
headlines.description: Greek swimmer Kristian Gkolomeev beats a world record in the pool at the controversial Enhanced Games in Las Vegas.

headlines.title: EasyJet flight diverts to Rome over power bank in luggage
headlines.description: Many airlines have toughened their rules on power banks, often requiring that they be stored in hand luggage not checked luggage.

headlines.title: Starmer 'appalled' by case of boys spared jail after raping teenage girls
headlines.description: The prime minister said it was "right" that the sentences were being urgently reviewed.

headlines.title: How Saudi Arabia's spending spree reached the end of the line
headlines.description: MBS's Vison 2030 project seemed the stuff of science fiction. Now reality has bitten

headlines.title: Education secretary to ask competition watchdog to review hidden childcare costs
headlines.description: Bridget Phillipson will ask the competition watchdog to examine costs that families are still facing in government-funded childcare.

headlines.title: Anger grows after China's deadliest coal mining disaster in years
headlines.description: On China's tightly-controlled internet, people are calling for justice and questioning how this happened.

headlines.title: Sun, superstars and other takeaways from Radio 1's Big Weekend
headlines.description: Olivia Dean, Zara Larsson and Fatboy Slim topped the bill - but it wasn't all about the music.

headlines.title: What to do if you think someone has heat exhaustion or heatstroke
headlines.description: Know the signs and what to do if someone is unwell in hot weather.

headlines.title: Goodbye Salah and Pep, hello new champions - the Premier League in review
headlines.description: BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty reviews every team's Premier League - and looks back at his pre-season predictions.

headlines.title: She was told to marry in a country which bans girls' education. So she got in a taxi and fled
headlines.description: Nearly five years on from Afghanistan's school ban, young women say they have waved goodbye to their dreams.

headlines.title: Beer boom goes flat as breweries call last orders
headlines.description: The UK's brewery scene is shrinking as pubs close, costs rise and drinking habits change.

headlines.title: Morocco wants tourists to visit Western Sahara. Some say it's tightening its control
headlines.description: The Moroccan government wants more Western holidaymakers to visit the territory it claims to own.

headlines.title: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured
headlines.description: A hypersonic missile, which reportedly travels over 10 times the speed of sound, was used, Russia has confirmed.

headlines.title: At least 19 people taken to hospital after 'strong smell' reported at Tokyo mall
headlines.description: Police say a man sprayed a substance at an ATM in the luxury Ginza 6 shopping centre.

headlines.title: Surf photographer attacked in water by 'shark or sea lion'
headlines.description: The finals day of the World Surf League's New Zealand Pro event was paused after a photographer was attacked.

headlines.title: First Hong Kong astronaut launches into space onboard Chinese mission
headlines.description: The 43-year-old police officer and mother of three serves as the team's payload scientist.

headlines.title: Raducanu exits French Open in first round - will she come out stronger at Wimbledon?
headlines.description: Emma Raducanu's French Open first-round defeat was not unexpected, but the nature of it may have left her wishing she had stayed at home.

headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The Louise Casey Interview (Live at Hay Festival)
headlines.description: Baroness Casey on violence against girls and whether governments take reviews seriously.

headlines.title: Americast

headlines.description: BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty reviews every team's Premier League - and looks back at his pre-season predictions.

headlines.title: Premier League predictions - how accurate were BBC Sport pundits?
headlines.description: Last summer, 33 BBC pundits - plus AI and Opta's 'supercomputer' were asked to pick which teams would finish in the Premier League's top four, and in which order. How did they get on?

headlines.title: The title battle finally came alive - then Antonelli took control
headlines.description: The Formula 1 title battle caught light in Canada - but then took a potentially decisive turn, writes Andrew Benson.

headlines.title: 'Spurs avoid humiliation but elation of fans will soon turn to anger'
headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur's celebrations in survival cannot cover up their embarrassment of a season that almost ended in the unthinkable, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

headlines.description: Emma Raducanu's French Open first-round defeat was not unexpected, but the nature of it may have left her wishing she had stayed at home.

headlines.title: What happens now at relegated West Ham?
headlines.description: West Ham's 14-year stay in the Premier League is over - but what changes lie ahead as they prepare for life in the Championship?
